title: Short-window time-series transform specialist for last-k lap shape
summary: Adjacent work on pit-stop prediction favors sequence models, and ROCKET/MiniRocket-style transforms are strong, cheap baselines for short ordered signals.
rationale: Adjacent work on pit-stop prediction favors sequence models, and ROCKET/MiniRocket-style transforms are strong, cheap baselines for short ordered signals. Your rows already form short causal histories, so a specialist on the last 4-8 laps may extract shape information that trees on point features miss.
implementation_hint: For each row, build causal per-driver sequences from recent `LapTime_Delta`, `LapTime (s)` residuals, degradation increments, position change, and pit history within the current stint; train a MiniRocket or MultiRocket classifier on those short sequences, then blend its OOF probabilities with the main tabular model.
expected_effect: Adds genuinely different temporal shape signal, especially for pace-falloff patterns that precede stops by a few laps rather than one.
risk: Sequence construction may be sparse at stint starts, and the gain can vanish if short histories are too noisy or poorly aligned across races.
